gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/342)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/342)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مَا أَدْنَى مَا يَجِي‏ءُ مِنَ الْإِسْرَافِ قَالَ ابْتِذَالُكَ ثَوْبَ صَوْنِكَ وَ إِهْرَاقُكَ فَضْلَ إِنَائِكَ وَ أَكْلُكَ التَّمْرَ وَ رَمْيَكَ بِالنَّوَى هَاهُنَا وَ هَاهُنَا .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Salih Bin Uqba, from Suleyman Bin Salih who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘What is the lowest of the extravagance?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Your routinely wearing of your protective clothing, and your discarding the remnants of your utensil, and your eating the dates and your throwing with the core over here and over there’.
en.sarwar: 2. Muhammad ibn Yahya Muhammad ibn al-Husayn from Muhammad ibn ’Isma‘il from Salih ibn ‘Uqbah from Sulayman ibn Salih who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the least degree of squandering. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is misuse of protective clothes, throwing away the remaining water in your bowl and when eating dates throwing the date-stone here and there.’”
